payload:
# ap — AI Coding Agent in Rust

Build `ap`, a terminal AI coding agent written in Rust with a ratatui TUI.

## Vision

`ap` is a first-class, extensible AI coding assistant that runs in the terminal. It should feel like a native tool — fast, composable, and hackable. Think of it as a spiritual sibling to `pi`, but in Rust, with a ratatui UI and a clean extension system baked in from day one.

## Core Requirements

### Language & Stack
- **Language:** Rust (stable toolchain)
- **TUI:** ratatu... [truncated, 6011 chars total]

Q1: When a pre_tool_call hook exits non-zero, does that cancel the tool call? If so, what does Claude receive — a tool_result with an error message? Can the hook stdout inject the cancellation reason? task_id=task-1774156270-b2c6 task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:requirements

Q1 answered: pre_tool_call hook cancellation protocol — non-zero exit cancels, stdout becomes tool_result error content, stderr is debug-only, fallback message if stdout empty. task_id=task-1774156270-b2c6 task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:requirements
Q2: parallel tool call execution — concurrent vs sequential? Same in TUI and -p mode? task_id=task-1774156270-b2c6 task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:requirements
Q2 answered: sequential tool execution in v1, same in both TUI and -p mode. task_id=task-1774156383-713b task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:requirements-answering

Q3: Can post_tool_call hooks modify the tool result sent to Claude (via stdout), or are they read-only? What env vars do they receive? task_id=task-1774156383-713b task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:requirements-answering

Q3 answered: post_tool_call hooks CAN modify tool results via stdout (non-empty stdout replaces content). Non-zero exit = warning+fallback to original (never blocks loop). Env vars: AP_TOOL_NAME, AP_TOOL_PARAMS, AP_TOOL_RESULT, AP_TOOL_IS_ERROR. task_id=task-1774156383-713b task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:requirements-answering

Q4: Are pre_turn/post_turn hooks read-only observers or can they modify messages? What env vars/data do they receive? task_id=task-1774156383-713b task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:requirements-answering

Q4 answered: pre_turn/post_turn are read-only observers in v1 (stdout ignored, non-zero exit = advisory warning only). Data delivered via temp file paths in env vars (AP_MESSAGES_FILE for pre_turn, AP_RESPONSE_FILE for post_turn). Modification at turn level deferred to v2 pre_turn_transform/post_turn_transform hooks. task_id=task-1774156383-713b task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:requirements-answering

ap-ai-coding-agent requirements finalized: hooks protocol (Q1-Q4), sequential tool execution, post_tool_call transform, pre/post_turn read-only observers with temp file data delivery. Spec is implementation-ready.

ap-ai-coding-agent design complete: sequential tools, hook protocols (gate/transform/observer), ratatui TUI with mpsc channels, extensions interface-only v1, full Mermaid diagrams. task_id=task-1774156825-ec07 task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:design-draft

3 FAILs: (1) Provider trait not defined in provider/mod.rs — agent loop coupled to Bedrock, blocks mock testing; (2) async trait approach unspecified — impl Future in trait return position breaks object-safety for Box<dyn Tool>; (3) Registry API incomplete — R6.3/R6.4 require hooks/panels/interception fields in v1 interface but design only has tools Vec. Also 5 CONCERNs: edit multiple-match undefined, bash timeout absent, hook-not-found unhandled, R4.3 cancel-one-keep-others not explicit in loop, AP_HAS_TOOL_USE missing from Appendix B. task_id=task-1774157005-4d71 task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:design-review

Q5: edit tool multiple-match — if old_text appears more than once, should the tool (a) replace first occurrence only, (b) replace all occurrences, or (c) return an error? task_id=task-1774157005-4d71 task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:design-review

Q5 answered: edit tool returns error when old_text matches >1 occurrence (count included in error message). Forces LLM precision. task_id=task-1774157005-4d71 task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:design-review

Q5 answered: edit tool returns error with count on multiple matches. All requirements gaps resolved. Architect must fix design FAILs: 1) define Provider trait, 2) specify BoxFuture for object-safe async Tool trait, 3) fix Registry API to include hooks/panels/intercept stubs. Also address C2-C5 concerns with safe defaults. task_id=task-1774157005-4d71 task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:design-review

design.md revised: FAIL-1 (Provider trait defined), FAIL-2 (BoxFuture for object-safe Tool trait), FAIL-3 (Registry includes tools/hooks/panels/interceptors v1 stubs). Concerns C1-C5 addressed. task_id=task-1774157005-4d71 task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:design-review

All 3 FAILs fixed: Provider trait defined, BoxFuture for object-safe Tool, Registry has all 4 surfaces (tools/hooks/panels/interceptors). All 5 Concerns addressed (C1-C5). 3 minor advisory notes for builder: on_error env var name, TUI async integration pattern, converse_stream alternative. Design is complete and implementable. task_id=task-1774157005-4d71 task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:design-review

User feedback: replace dylib-only extension stub with first-class Rhai scripting support in v1. Rhai extensions in ~/.ap/extensions/*.rhai register tools via script interface (name/description/schema/execute fns). Rust wraps as Box<dyn Tool>. Dylib loading deferred to v2. See scratchpad for full spec.

Correction: both Rhai (.rhai) AND Rust dylib (.dylib/.so via libloading) extensions ship in v1 — neither is a stub. Dylib entry point: extern C fn ap_extension_init(registry: *mut Registry). Discovery scans for both file types. See scratchpad.

Design amended: extensions system fully rewritten for v1 — both Rhai scripting (rhai crate, .rhai files, RhaiTool wrapper) and Rust dylib loading (libloading, C ABI ap_extension_init) are first-class. Neither is a stub. See section 4.8, Appendix C/D/E for full details. All prior FAILs and Concerns remain fixed.

Extensions amendment has 3 compile-time FAILs: (1) rhai::Engine is !Send+!Sync — needs rhai features=[sync] in Cargo.toml; (2) loader.rs extension() match on Option<&OsStr> vs &str literals won't compile; (3) Library handles from load_dylib are dropped immediately causing use-after-free — no Vec<Library> storage defined. task_id=task-1774157005-4d71 task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:design-review

All 3 extension FAILs are Architect-level fixes, no user input needed: (1) add rhai features=[sync] to Cargo.toml; (2) fix OsStr match pattern with .and_then(|e| e.to_str()); (3) add Vec<Library> field to store dylib handles. Requirements complete — Architect to revise design.md.

Fixed all 3 extension FAILs: (1) rhai features=[sync] for Engine Send+Sync; (2) OsStr match via .and_then(|e| e.to_str()); (3) ExtensionLoader{libraries: Vec<Library>} to prevent dlclose use-after-free. Strengthened dylib safety warning (New C4). Ready for Design Critic 4th pass.

4th pass: all 3 FAIL-NEWs resolved (rhai sync feature, OsStr match, ExtensionLoader library retention). All prior FAILs/Concerns remain fixed. 2 minor non-blocking concerns (stale requirements.md R6.4, Rhai sandbox API unspecified). Design is complete and implementable. task_id=task-1774157005-4d71 task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:design-review

12-step TDD implementation plan for ap-ai-coding-agent created at .agents/scratchpad/implementation/ap-ai-coding-agent/plan.md — Wave 1: scaffold + config; includes unit/integration/E2E test strategy and MockProvider pattern

task: impl:ap:task-01-scaffold (Cargo.toml + Project Scaffold). cargo build --release passes, ap --version prints 'ap 0.1.0', all deps declared, ap.toml.example has all 4 sections, 2 unit tests pass. Code at ap/.

task: impl:ap:task-01-scaffold — all AC met: cargo build --release clean, ap --version=0.1.0, ap.toml.example has 4 sections, all deps declared (rhai sync feature, libloading, etc.), 2 tests pass, clippy clean, adversarial paths safe
task-01-scaffold complete: cargo build --release clean, ap --version=0.1.0, all deps declared, 2 tests pass. Next wave: task-02 config system.

task-02 config system materialized: task_id=task-1774159584-7ba2, key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:step-02:config-system, file=.agents/scratchpad/implementation/ap-ai-coding-agent/tasks/task-02-config-system.code-task.md

task_id=task-1774159584-7ba2, task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:step-02:config-system, file=.agents/scratchpad/implementation/ap-ai-coding-agent/tasks/task-02-config-system.code-task.md, files_changed=ap/src/config.rs (new), ap/src/main.rs (updated), tests=5/5 config tests pass + 7/7 total, build=cargo build --release zero warnings, commit=b2e14ba

task_id=task-1774159584-7ba2, task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:step-02:config-system, file=tasks/task-02-config-system.code-task.md — all 5 acceptance criteria met, 7/7 tests pass, zero clippy warnings, field-level overlay merge logic correct

task-1774159584-7ba2 (pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:step-02:config-system) complete — AppConfig with field-level overlay merge, 5 config tests pass, 7 total tests pass, zero clippy warnings. Advance to Step 3: Tool trait + 4 built-in tools.

Step 3 runtime task materialized: task-1774159929-32ea (pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:step-03:tool-trait-builtin-tools) — Tool trait + 4 built-in tools (read, write, edit, bash), 12 unit tests, EditTool multi-match error, BashTool no timeout. Backing file: .agents/scratchpad/implementation/ap-ai-coding-agent/tasks/task-03-tool-trait-builtin-tools.code-task.md
task_id=task-1774159929-32ea task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:step-03:tool-trait-builtin-tools files=ap/src/tools/{mod,read,write,edit,bash}.rs tests=25 passed build=clean commit=def2c6b

task_id=task-1774159929-32ea task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:step-03:tool-trait-builtin-tools file=ap/src/tools/bash.rs — all 26 tests pass, zero warnings; bash format fix correct (explicit \n between stdout/stderr), regression test guards it; all 4 tools implemented with correct behavior, object-safe Tool trait, ToolRegistry complete
task-1774159929-32ea (step-03 tool-trait-builtin-tools) complete: 26 tests pass, zero warnings, all 4 tools + ToolRegistry implemented. Next: step-04 Bedrock provider.

step-04 wave: task-1774160297-6f5f (pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:step-04:provider-trait-bedrock) — implement Provider trait + BedrockProvider with streaming. See tasks/task-04-provider-trait-bedrock.code-task.md
task_id=task-1774160297-6f5f task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:step-04:provider-trait-bedrock files=ap/src/provider/mod.rs,ap/src/provider/bedrock.rs tests=8 new (34 total) build=clean commit=6c23f33

task_id=task-1774160297-6f5f task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:step-04:provider-trait-bedrock file=ap/src/provider/bedrock.rs bug=content_block_stop emits ToolUseEnd unconditionally — Anthropic API fires content_block_stop for text blocks too, causing spurious ToolUseEnd on every plain-text response. Fix: add 'let mut in_tool_use = false' state variable; set true in content_block_start when block type is tool_use; only emit ToolUseEnd in content_block_stop when in_tool_use is true, then reset to false.

task_id=task-1774160297-6f5f task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:step-04:provider-trait-bedrock file=ap/src/provider/bedrock.rs fix=extracted parse_sse_event() helper with in_tool_use:&mut bool state; content_block_stop only emits ToolUseEnd when in_tool_use==true, then resets; 6 new targeted tests added; 40 tests total pass; zero warnings

task_id=task-1774160297-6f5f task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:step-04:provider-trait-bedrock file=ap/src/provider/bedrock.rs — all acceptance criteria met: StreamEvent/ProviderError types correct per design, parse_sse_event() helper extracted with in_tool_use guard, content_block_stop correctly gates ToolUseEnd, 6 new targeted SSE tests + 8 existing = 14 provider tests pass, 40 total pass, zero warnings, clean release build

# ap — AI Coding Agent in Rust

Build `ap`, a terminal AI coding agent written in Rust with a ratatui TUI.

## Vision

`ap` is a first-class, extensible AI coding assistant that runs in the terminal. It should feel like a native tool — fast, composable, and hackable. Think of it as a spiritual sibling to `pi`, but in Rust, with a ratatui UI and a clean extension system baked in from day one.

task: pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:step-04:provider-trait-bedrock — Provider trait + BedrockProvider complete. 40 tests pass, zero warnings. BoxStream<StreamEvent>, parse_sse_event unit-tested, build_messages/build_request_body tested.

task: pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:step-04:provider-trait-bedrock (task-1774160297-6f5f) — Provider trait + BedrockProvider complete. 40/40 tests pass, zero warnings, zero clippy. BoxStream object-safe, parse_sse_event in_tool_use state machine correct, build_messages/build_request_body tested. task-1774160297-6f5f (pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:step-04:provider-trait-bedrock) complete. Provider trait + BedrockProvider implemented. BoxStream object-safe, parse_sse_event state machine correct, build_messages/build_request_body tested. 40/40 tests pass, zero warnings. Ready for step 05: Hooks system.

Step 05 runtime task materialized: task-1774189460-bd51 (pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:step-05:hooks-system). Implement HookRunner with pre_tool_call/post_tool_call/run_observer_hook, 6 unit tests. Backing: task-05-hooks-system.code-task.md

task_id=task-1774189460-bd51 task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:step-05:hooks-system files=ap/src/hooks/mod.rs,ap/src/hooks/runner.rs,ap/src/main.rs tests=6/6 hook tests pass, 46 total pass build=clean zero warnings commit=8245fab

task_id=task-1774189460-bd51 task_key=pdd:ap-ai-coding-agent:step-05:hooks-system file=ap/src/hooks/runner.rs tests=48/48 build=clean. All 6 ACs met, adversarial temp-file integration test added and passes, no issues found.

task-1774189460-bd51 (step-05: hooks system) review.passed and closed. Tasks 01-05 complete. Tasks 06-11 (extensions, agent loop, sessions, TUI, non-interactive, README) remain pending. Materialize next wave.
